changes.before: He read like a man running the same line twice because the first pass had not stuck.
changes.after: He ran the same line twice because the first pass had not stuck.

changes.before: Sylvanas could feel the strain coming off her in waves.
changes.after: Strain came off her in waves.

changes.before: "Before that the grain supply chain was normal. Predictable origins, consistent volume, the same carriers on the same routes."
changes.after: "Before that the grain chain was normal. Predictable origins, consistent volume, the same carriers on the same routes."

changes.before: She heard the pull in that at once.
changes.after: The pull in it was obvious at once.

changes.before: His face barely changed, but Sylvanas saw it.
changes.after: His face barely changed, but the change was there.

changes.before: He moved through the district like a man moving through his own house.
changes.after: He moved through the district with a householder's certainty.

changes.before: Then she kept walking as if she had seen nothing worth stopping for.
changes.after: Then she kept walking without breaking stride.

changes.before: She touched the bowstring without thinking and saw the handler.
changes.after: She touched the bowstring without thinking. Then the handler stepped out.

changes.before: He moved like a man going somewhere and looked at nothing.
changes.after: He moved with purpose and looked at nothing.

changes.before: He kept walking as if the hand had never moved.
Through the gap where the dock doors did not quite meet, she saw enough.
changes.after: Through the gap where the dock doors did not quite meet, the view gave her enough.

changes.before: The room smelled of tallow, old paper, and the sour trace of grain dust that clung to everything in this city.
changes.after: The room smelled of tallow, old paper, and the sour trace of grain dust.

changes.before: "The design accounts for every step between sack and table."
changes.after: "The design accounts for every step between sack and table."

changes.before: She watched his hand on the map.
changes.after: His hand stayed on the map.

changes.before: His map lay open on the table like an argument not won.
changes.after: His map lay open on the table, still unfinished.
Removed break and ran the operation as one continuous movement.
Removed break and kept the return briefing inside the same active line.

observations.title: Read weapons and target
items: Config confirmed primary mode for chapter7 uses v1.md -> v2.md and requires a reduction pass rather than revision-mode patching.
Story arc places chapter7 at the Andorhal turn where the procedural hunt hardens into larger conspiracy, so the prose needed darker efficiency without changing plot sequence.
Sylvanas voice sheet requires tactical noticing and clipped judgments; I cut explanatory feeling-language that stepped outside that frame.
Jaina voice sheet allows fast exact reasoning but forbids modern lab jargon; I kept her plague analysis technical without turning it into a report.
Anti-slop corpus loaded sentence-level and paragraph-level quirk files before editing; the main risk surface was filters, over-explanation, opener monotony, and document voice.

observations.title: Research keywords
items: Canon check matched Andorhal to Lordaeron's grain-distribution hub, so the chapter's granary focus and plague-routing logic stayed intact.
Location notes matched the city's expected feel: grain dust, clerks, manifests, loading yards, and practical low-walled infrastructure.
Dalaran and Kirin Tor research supported Corin's ledger culture and the inspection-cover premise; no canon correction was needed there.
Pre-undeath Sylvanas research matched Farstrider spacing, rooftop pursuit, and border-commander temperament, so those beats were preserved rather than softened.
Pre-Theramore Jaina research matched precise plague analysis plus moral seriousness under pressure; her strongest lines stayed exact but human.

observations.title: Wordcount reduction
items: Overall wordcount dropped from 9404 to 6566, a 30.2 percent cut, while keeping the stable approach, gate entry, office briefing, rooftop tail, return briefing, and operation assignment.
Opening approach paragraphs were compressed by stripping repeat commentary around competence cues while keeping Farstrider spacing, Jaina's strain, and the clerk checkpoint.
The city-arrival section lost layered restatement about Andorhal's false normality; the full fact set remained but the emphasis now lands faster.
Office briefing paragraphs were tightened hardest, especially repeated explanations of Havel's control instinct and Corin's exhaustion.
Return briefing and planning lost duplicated suspicion beats and repeated summaries of the same map logic.

observations.title: Hitlist patterns
items: Actor-demotion was reduced in lines such as the clerk reading papers and Havel's map hand staying fixed, putting deliberate action back on people instead of abstractions.
Filter scaffolds were cut from surveillance beats and emotional beats: watched/saw/heard constructions were replaced where they only delayed the image.
Both gratuitous scene-break markers were removed because the chapter never truly hard-cuts; it stays on one continuous operational line.
Simile density was cut from 16 like + 1 as if in v1 to 2 like + 0 as if in v2, with the weakest camera-move comparisons removed first.
Placeholder phrasing dropped sharply: 'something' fell from 22 to 6 by cashing out signals into doors, hands, lanes, masks, and routes.

observations.title: Dialogue register hunt
items: Corin's explanation kept the ledger facts, but surplus institutional phrasing was pared back so he sounds like an exhausted junior mage, not an audit memo.
Jaina's plague explanation kept 'outer shell' and repeated-ingestion logic while avoiding banned lab-report diction from the hitlist.
Havel's lines stayed militia-practical; I removed narration that over-interpreted his speech instead of letting the bluntness carry it.
Operational dialogue now cashes out into usable objects and timings: service door, third bell, north lane, south rooftop, loading dock, exits.
Targeted search found no remaining zero-tolerance dialogue offenders such as actionable, vector, optimal, propagation, substrate, concentration, or ingested.

flags: Line 35: kept 'Grain dust lay in the cobble joints like pale sand' because it is a concrete visual simile and the chapter is now under the simile cap.
Line 315: kept Jaina's 'designed' conclusion because the chapter needs the explicit shift from contamination to weaponization.
canonChecks: Andorhal remained the grain-distribution hub and plague insertion point.
Dalaran and Kirin Tor scholarly culture remained consistent with the cover story and Corin's role.
Sylvanas stayed pre-undeath Farstrider-tactical rather than banshee-styled.
Jaina stayed pre-Theramore exact, humane, and anti-brutality.
